title: Japanese lender launches Bitcoin-backed loans of up to $6.2M
summary: CRYL is offering Bitcoin-backed loans to individuals and businesses as Japanese firms explore broader uses for BTC in lending and credit markets.

title: TeraWulf eyes $3.5B debt raise for Anthropic-linked data center: Report
summary: Bitcoin miner TeraWulf is reportedly seeking $3.5 billion in debt financing led by Morgan Stanley for its Kentucky data center campus leased by AI company Anthropic.

title: Corn Drops as Heat Threat Eases Ahead of Key US Supply Report
summary: Corn futures fell for a third day as the immediate threat of extreme heat in the US eased. Traders are also awaiting the US Department of Agriculture’s WASDE report, due later Friday.

title: Inflation Is a Tailwind for European, Morgan Stanley Says
summary: European equities stand to benefit from higher inflation, according to Morgan Stanley’s Marina Zavolock, who says the market underestimates the region’s exposure to sectors that profit from rising prices. title: Jailed Fraudster Charged With Moving $290K in Forfeited Crypto From Prison
summary: A convicted launderer serving time for a $5 million scheme allegedly moved crypto that a court had ordered him to forfeit.

title: North Carolina Bill Recognizes CFTC's ‘Federal Regulatory Authority’ Over Prediction Markets
summary: The state's budget law leaves oversight of Kalshi and Polymarket to the CFTC and taxes them at 6%, far below what other states are pursuing.

title: Stablecoin firm Circle wins final OCC approval to open national trust bank
summary: Circle wins final OCC approval for First National Digital Currency Bank, with USDC reserve management planned for a later phase.

title: Ledger researchers disclose Tangem card flaw; Tangem says risk to everyday users is ‘virtually non-existent’
summary: Ledger researchers disclosed a laser attack that resets Tangem card passwords by bypassing a recovery-state check in the firmware.

title: OpenAI and Google sell AI models to blacklisted China groups
summary: US groups have been supplying AI services to Singapore-based subsidiaries of Alibaba, Baidu and Tencent

title: Tencent leads deal to unwind Meta’s $2bn Manus acquisition
summary: Chinese tech group set to become largest shareholder in AI agent start-up after Beijing ordered reversal of US takeover
